stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Ending war before winter ‘correct and realistic’, says senior Kyiv aide
stories.summary: Budanov backs Zelenskyy call to capitalise on Kyiv’s strong position on battlefield; Kyiv under heavy attack into Tuesday morning. What we know on day 1,560 A deal to end the war against Russia by winter is a “realistic” outcome , Kyrylo Budanov said. The celebrated former Ukrainian spymaster, now Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s chief of staff, said on Monday: “This is the president’s instruction: to try to end this war as soon as possible … preferably before winter. In my opinion, this is absolutely corr

stories.title: Massive Russian air attack on Ukraine kills at least 12 people
stories.summary: Kyiv was targeted in a large-scale Russian strike Tuesday, just over a week after another big attack on Ukraine’s capital. The missile and drone barrage killed at least 12 people, Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said on X. The authorities reported an additional 58 people had been injured. Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said the attack damaged or destroyed dozens of residential buildings, including four medical centers. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had warned Friday that fresh Russia

stories.title: Orbán’s oligarchs on edge as Hungary poised to launch wealth tax
stories.summary: New PM Péter Magyar calls policy a sign of ‘social justice’ after years of political loyalty being rewarded with economic opportunity In a dimly lit television studio, one of Hungary’s richest men is on the verge of tears. It is early May, weeks after the general election that ended Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power, and the advertising mogul Balásy Gyula has an announcement to make. Gyula tells the interviewer that he has just surrendered his businesses to the state, along with a chunk of hi

stories.title: Cyclist praised for rescuing four people who nearly drowned in Ruhr, Germany
stories.summary: Family were enjoying a waterside barbecue in Bochum before members ended up in jeopardy in river A cyclist has been praised for having “stepped in decisively” and rescuing four members of a family who nearly drowned in the Ruhr River in Germany during a waterside barbecue that almost ended in tragedy. The family of eight had set themselves up on the riverbank in the western city of Bochum, the local fire brigade reported, but the gathering took a panicked turn when one woman got too close to the

stories.title: Germany news: Poverty rises to new high, charity group says
stories.summary: Poverty in Germany appears to be rising, with one charity group warning of a "crisislike situation." Meanwhile, Hungary's new prime minister, Peter Magyar, is visiting Chancellor Friedrich Merz in Berlin. DW has more.

stories.title: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
stories.summary: Hungary's president has refused Prime Minister Péter Magyar's demands to step aside, setting up a constitutional clash.

stories.title: Lithuania turns pink for giant cold soup festival
stories.summary: More than 100,000 people are expected to attend Vilnius’s Pink Soup Festival, a three-day celebration of Lithuania’s famous cold beet soup featuring races, costumes, foam slides and mass communal lunches.

stories.title: Olyan rossz a magyar intézmények állapota, hogy ez most már komoly növekedési tartalék
stories.summary: A magyar gazdaság problémáit általában az inflációval, a gyenge növekedéssel vagy az államháztartási hiánnyal szoktuk magyarázni. Pedig van egy ennél mélyebb és hosszabb távon súlyosabb folyamat is: az ország intézményi környezetének romlása.

stories.title: Kedden Budapestre érkezik az EU jogállami biztosa
stories.summary: Ahogy tavaly, úgy idén is több magyar miniszterrel egyeztet Magyarországon Michael McGrath, mielőtt az Európai Bizottság kiadná az éves jogállamisági jelentését.

stories.title: Magyar Péter az EU két legfontosabb országába látogat, ahol Orbánék terhelt viszonyt hagytak hátra
stories.summary: Kedden Berlinben, szerdán Párizsban tárgyal a miniszterelnök, aki a Merzcel és Macronnal tervezett találkozókról azt mondta, hogy dolgoznak tovább Magyarország helyének erősítésén Európában.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt a szívhangrendeletről: Nem a szakma mondta azt, hogy bizonyítottan szükséges a szívhang hallhatóvá tétele
stories.summary: Az egészségügyi miniszter hosszú bejegyzésben magyarázta el, mi a baja a szívhangrendelettel.

stories.title: Megérkezett a Koin, ami új korszakot nyit a digitális pénzügyekben
stories.summary: A Bank360 Koin egyetlen felületen gyűjti össze a különböző bankoknál vezetett számlák adatait, kategorizálja a tranzakciókat, és átláthatóbbá teszi a napi pénzügyeket. Mennyire érett erre a magyar piac, mit változtathat a bankolási szokásokon, és hol van ebben a Mastercard szerepe? Erről beszélgettünk Berkes Tiborral, a Mastercard közép-európai divíziójának termékfejlesztési vezetőjével, valamint Turmezey Tamással és Surányi Leventével, a Bank360 alapítóival.

stories.title: SOILATHON: nem lesz termés jó talaj nélkül, verseny indul a megoldásokért (x)
stories.summary: A klímaváltozásról szóló vitákban sok szó esik a vízről, az időjárási szélsőségekről vagy az üvegházhatású gázok kibocsátásának csökkentéséről. Közben van egy erőforrás, amire jóval kevesebb figyelem jut, pedig a magyar mezőgazdaság jövője is nagyrészt ezen múlik: a talaj.

stories.title: Magyar threatens to oust President Sulyok by amending constitution
stories.summary: As the Orbán-appointed Tamás Sulyok refuses to resign, here's what the next step of the Tisza Party's supermajority might look like.

stories.title: 35 éve a Nestlénél – Noszek Péter ügyvezető igazgató volt a vendégünk
stories.summary: A Nestlé Hungária top 100-as, exportvolument tekintve top 10-es cég Magyarországon, a PwC elemzése alapján éves szinten 0,5 százalékkal járul hozzá a magyar gazdaság működéséhez. Noszek Péter ügyvezető igazgató volt a vendégünk, aki már 35 éve dolgozik a cégnél. Kérdeztük a hazai gyártásról és a magyar piac sajátosságairól, de azt az érdekességet is megosztotta velünk, hogy milyen véletlen vezetett ahhoz, hogy a részese lett a Nestlé kelet-európai piacra lépésének 1991-ben.

stories.title: Egyszer csak a fejemen landolt egy kerti bútor – ilyen volt Travis Scott budapesti bulija
stories.summary: Van, akinek minden pénzt megért, más vegyes érzésekkel távozott Travis Scott káoszos csepeli bulijáról. A helyszínen a fanatikus rajongók mellett T. Danny, Manuel és a helikopterrel bereptetett Beton.Hofi is nyilatkozott a Telexnek.

stories.title: Orbán Anita először a holland külügyminisztert fogadta hivatalos látogatáson
stories.summary: A legfontosabb téma az Erasmus-ösztöndíjak voltak.

stories.title: Júliusban érkezik Charli XCX új albuma, aminek a borítóján Martin Scorsese is befigyel
stories.summary: A legendás filmrendező mellett John Cale zeneszerző és Marc Jacobs divattervező szerepelnek a lemezborítón.

stories.title: A kétharmados többség bármit megtehet – kreatív jogászkodással távolíthatják el a köztársasági elnököt
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás nem mondott le a határidőig, és a miniszterelnök bejelentette: Alaptörvény-módosítással mozdítják el a pozíciójából, de hogy hogyan, azt egyelőre a frakciójának se árulta el. Az Alaptörvény módosítása felett nincs tartalmi kontroll, így a tiszások szinte bármit beleírhatnak a szövegbe.

stories.title: Távozik Szentkirályi Alexandra a Fidesz budapesti szervezetének éléről
stories.summary: Az új budapesti elnök Szatmáry Kristóf lesz, aki a választáson 30 százalékponttal kapott ki a választókerületében.

stories.title: Oroszi Beatrix epidemiológus lett az új országos tisztifőorvos
stories.summary: Az új kormány célja a profiltisztítás, kiveszik a gyógyszerfelügyeletet a népegészségügyi központból.

stories.title: What Hungary must do to receive EU funds frozen under Orban
stories.summary: Prime Minister Peter Magyar and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a deal that could free up over €16 billion in EU funds for Hungary. Now, Magyar's new government needs to deliver.

stories.title: Could Nato be dragged into Ukraine-Russia war? – The Latest
stories.summary: Nato has vowed to ‘defend every inch’ of its territory after a Russian drone hit an apartment building in Romania. The strike prompted swift condemnation and threats of repercussion from European leaders. So is there a risk the war could expand beyond Ukraine? Lucy Hough speaks to senior international correspondent Peter Beaumont Continue reading...

stories.title: EU unblocks €16 billion in Hungary assistance as Magyar promises reforms
stories.summary: Reforms under new Prime Minister Peter Magyar will enable the EU to free up billions in frozen assistance for Budapest. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen announced the news as Magyar visited Brussels.

stories.title: Romania's political crisis deepens
stories.summary: After Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan’s government fell in early May, Romania's moderate parties have struggled to chart a new course — opening the door for the far right to gain ground.

stories.title: Trump reportedly mulling retreat from $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund
stories.summary: Democrats decry president’s ‘most brazen act of self-dealing yet’ and vow to challenge fund in Congress Donald Trump is reconsidering whether to keep pressing for a $1.8bn fund to compensate his allies, a person familiar with his thinking said on Monday, as the justice department paused the program to comply with a court order. Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund has faced legal setbacks since it was announced two weeks ago. The idea has also faced a mounting political backlash from Republicans co

stories.title: Middle East updates: US-Iran talks appear to stall
stories.summary: An Iranian military official says "war is inevitable" because Tehran won't surrender. Also, reports of strikes between Israel and Hezbollah continue after President Donald Trump said "shooting will stop." DW has more.

stories.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
stories.summary: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June. “This event wil

stories.title: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
stories.summary: About 800 Mozambicans said to be caught up in violence in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration protests sweep country Five Mozambique nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend, the Mozambican government said – the first deaths officially linked to protests against illegal immigration sweeping the country. About 800 Mozambican nationals were caught up in violence that broke out in the southern coastal city of Mossel Bay on Friday, a government statement said. Continue

stories.title: Federal government paid $3.8m in personal protection for then CFMEU administrator
stories.summary: Senate committee also hears Murray Watt needed personal security after sending the union into administration Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The federal government has paid millions of dollars in personal protection for the administrators of the CFMEU, and had to organise security for minister Murray Watt after he introduced the legislation that placed the union into administration, it has been revealed. Under questioning from Liberal senator Jane Hume in the Senate

stories.title: No 10 urged to review religious knife rules after Henry Nowak murder
stories.summary: Hampshire police commissioner writes to Keir Starmer over ‘national tragedy’ of Southampton stabbing last December UK politics live – latest updates The police and crime commissioner for Hampshire is leading calls for a review of religious exemptions on the carrying of knives after Henry Nowak, 18, was murdered by a man carrying a “Sikh dagger” in Southampton. Donna Jones described the stabbing of the university student as a “national tragedy” and said she was writing to the prime minister, Keir

stories.title: Massive overnight Russian missiles rock Ukraine, killing at least 11 people, authorities say
stories.summary: The latest attacks come days after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that Russia was preparing a "massive new strike" on the country.

stories.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
stories.summary: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU trade lawmakers back compromise on deal with Trump
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Parliament’s trade committee approved by a wide majority a compromise struck with EU governments and the Commission on legislation to implement a trade deal with the United States. Lawmakers backed, by 31 votes to six, with three abstentions, changes to legislation under which the EU agreed to eliminate tariffs on U.S. industrial goods and some agricultural products — a key pillar of the agreement struck last summer at Donald Trump’s golf resort in Turnberry, Scotland. Eu

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new migrant return law
stories.summary: Negotiators on Monday agreed new rules to speed up and increase deportations from the EU, including making it possible to send failed asylum-seekers to hubs outside the bloc. The text is part of sweeping reforms the EU is rolling out to increase control over who crosses its external borders and to support countries that receive the most external migrants, with Monday’s agreement landing just days before other migration and asylum reforms start applying on June 12. Monday’s agreement

stories.title: Pentagon bars journalists from entering its press office citing re-designation
stories.summary: Revocation of access newest attempt by Trump’s defense department to restrict reporting on military affairs Journalists may no longer enter the Pentagon’s press office, which has been designated as a classified space amid growing moves to restrict press access to the defense department. “This is the most transparent war department in history. No amount of spin from the Fake News media will change that,” Jose Valdez, the acting defense department press secretary, said in a social media post. “The

stories.title: UN experts warn against ‘surging Israeli settler terror’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. See our full report: Trump says Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to ‘stop all shooting’ The exchange of strikes between the US and Iran reflects the fragility of the current ceasefire, which has seen repeated violations even as American and Iranian officials try to negotiate a deal to extend it. Iran has maintained its chokehold on the strait of Hormuz, disrupting global energy supplies as a fifth of all oil and natural gas traded once passed through the narrow mouth of

stories.title: Victorian minister regrets character references for taxi driver who assaulted women, DV accused men and Iran regime supporter
stories.summary: Labor’s Luba Grigorovitch wrote 33 character references but says she regrets providing them for six individuals and will never write another Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Newly promoted Victorian government minister Luba Grigorovitch has apologised for writing 33 character references – including for a taxi driver convicted of assaulting female passengers, two men accused of family violence and a supporter of fo

stories.title: About 3 million workers on minimum wage to receive 4.75% pay rise in Fair Work Commission ruling
stories.summary: Unions had demanded 6% pay increase for lowest paid after war in Middle East pushed inflation higher Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast About 100,000 of the country’s lowest paid employees will receive an above-inflation pay rise of 6% from July, as part of the Fair Work Commission’s annual review that also delivered a 4.75% boost to 2.7 million workers on award wages. As the government and unions welcomed the decis

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith won’t see full brief of war crime allegations for months – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The foreign minister, Penny Wong , has announced new sanctions by the commonwealth over three Israeli individuals and four entities in response to “escalating settler violence against Palestinians in the West Bank”. The entities will receive targeted financial sanctions while the individuals will receive travel bans to Australia. For the first time, designated entities now include farming outposts that serve as

stories.title: Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine
stories.summary: At least 14 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian cities Volodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 14 people and injured dozens more. Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, the central cities of Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia, and

stories.title: ‘Not the deal promised’: Labor’s Ed Husic questions Aukus pact that will deliver secondhand subs
stories.summary: Former frontbencher launches most significant internal criticism of $368bn Aukus deal since ALP national conference in 2023 Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia needs a backup plan for the Aukus submarine agreement, Labor MP Ed Husic has warned, arguing sluggish US production and the “transactional nature” of the Trump administration have put the multibillion-dollar defence deal at risk. The defence minister

stories.title: Murrell used false accounting and fake invoices to hide SNP embezzlement, court hears – UK politics live
stories.summary: Items bought by former chief executive included more than £23,000 from a luxury stationery brand and a £3,000 robotic lawnmower BBC Scotland has more details of the Peter Murrell hearing this morning on its live blog. And, on its live blog , Sky News has pictures of some of the items purchased by Murrell with stolen SNP funds. Andy Burnham will not call an early election if he becomes prime minister after the Makerfield byelection, a spokesperson for the Greater Manchester mayor has said. Contin

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Conflict continues in Lebanon despite Trump hailing Israeli-Hezbollah de-escalation
stories.summary: Israeli military says it intercepted missiles from Lebanon this morning while Netanyahu says his forces will continue operating in the south of the country Trump says Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to ‘stop all shooting’ The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) has once again issued a warning telling people in the southern Lebanese city of Nabatieh to flee their homes and move north ahead of strikes. It is the third such warning in a week for one of southern Lebanon’s largest cities, home to tens of th

stories.title: Russia launches major missile attack on Ukraine, several killed
stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy had been warning residents for days that Russia was planning a major assault. Moscow said it carried out a "massive" strike.

stories.title: India news: Mamata Banerjee presses ahead with protest amid turmoil in TMC
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee said she would proceed with a sit-in protest in Kolkata, amid escalating tensions within the TMC. DW has more.

stories.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
stories.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

stories.title: Six people killed in Iowa domestic dispute, police say
stories.summary: The suspected shooter was found dead near a bridge with a self-inflicted gunshot wound, authorities say.

stories.title: Influencers sought to sell Cambridge homes in China
stories.summary: An estate agent is looking for tech-savvy students to help find buyers in China and Hong Kong.

stories.title: France seized sanctioned Russian oil tanker with UK help, Macron says
stories.summary: A British helicopter provided support during the operation on Sunday, the UK ministry of defence says.

stories.title: UN urges calm as Israel advances into Lebanon and Iran threatens counter-fronts
stories.summary: The UN has called on all sides to respect the ceasefire following an Israeli military push into Lebanon, which threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks. Israel ordered strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Hezbollah targeted Haifa, prompting Iran to threaten the opening of new fronts.

stories.title: Minister missing from Mandelson files recalls ‘nightmare’ of stolen phone
stories.summary: LONDON — A British minister whose messages with Peter Mandelson are absent from the public disclosures forced by the Jeffrey Epstein scandal recounted Tuesday the “nightmare” of having his phone stolen. In an interview with Sky News, Nick Thomas-Symonds — the Cabinet minister overseeing the U.K.’s “reset” in relations with the EU — described how one of a group of three people on mopeds swiped his device last Oct. 15 while he was walking near the Home Office. “Unfortun

stories.title: White Castle adds veggie burger to menu as plant-based eating grows
stories.summary: Ohio-based slider chain introduces Southwest Veggie Slider permanently after customer feedback for meatless option Vegetarians have a new reason to celebrate: White Castle has added a permanent meat-free option to its menu. On Monday, the Columbus, Ohio -based slider chain announced the launch of the Southwest Veggie Slider, featuring a crispy brown rice crust and a smoky chipotle- and barbecue-inspired veggie patty made with sweet potatoes, black beans, corn, red bell peppers, onions and carrot

stories.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni return to court a month after reaching settlement
stories.summary: Lively’s legal team are suing her It Ends with Us co-star for legal fees and damages, reigniting a years-long court battle Attorneys for US actor Blake Likely were back in front of a New York judge on Monday to demand legal fees and damages from It Ends with Us co-star Justin Baldoni, after a settlement was reached last month in their years-long legal battle. The 38-year-old actor’s legal team argued that the defamation lawsuit brought against her by Baldoni and his production company, Wayfarer

stories.title: ‘They take you out of life, out of time’: a journey into Spain’s astonishing cave paintings
stories.summary: For tens of thousands of years, these Palaeolithic artworks were unseen. When they were rediscovered, onlookers marvelled at their vivid beauty. One of the world’s leading experts took me up close The aurochs, the mammoth and the steppe bison are long extinct, but their painted likenesses still look relatively fresh across the walls and roofs of Altamira. Or so said Diego Garate Maidagan, who is one of the very few humans allowed to enter that exalted cave in northern Spain. I met Garate last su

stories.title: Iowa shootings: gunman suspected of killing six relatives then himself
stories.summary: Police say four found dead in Muscatine home and two elsewhere in apparent ‘domestic-related dispute’ and suspect took own life while speaking to officers Authorities in Iowa are investigating the fatal shootings of six people who they believe were killed by a relative who took his own life when confronted by police. Police were called Monday to a home in Muscatine, about 50 miles (80km) south-east of Cedar Rapids, where they found four people fatally shot, Muscatine police chief Anthony Kies sa

stories.title: London tube strike causes huge disruption, with many services hit
stories.summary: About half of London Underground drivers take action on Tuesday, with second strike scheduled for Thursday A 24-hour strike by London Underground drivers has begun, causing huge disruption to tube services and on the roads. About half of London’s tube drivers are taking part in the action in a dispute over the introduction of a four-day working week. A second strike is planned for Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas
stories.summary: Exclusive: First shipwrecks found in Nassau harbour on New Providence, once the hideout of Blackbeard and Calico Jack The first shipwrecks linked to the real pirates of the Caribbean in the Bahamas have been discovered by an international team co-directed by a British marine archaeologist. Blackbeard and Calico Jack Rackham were among pirates who, between the 1690s and 1720s, turned Nassau on the island of New Providence into a hideout where they plotted their next heists on the high seas and di

stories.title: UK government has failed Palestinian people, says senior Labour MP
stories.summary: Emily Thornberry criticises Israel’s ‘staggering’ sense of impunity and rebukes Donald Trump for abandoning Gaza The UK government has let the Palestinian people down and failed to make it economically impossible for Israel to continue to act with impunity in the West Bank and Gaza, the Labour chair of the foreign affairs select committee, Emily Thornberry, has said. She accused her own government of lacking ambition and wringing its hands on the Palestinian crisis, and she also chastised Donald

stories.title: Body found in search for boy, 11, who went missing in South Yorkshire river
stories.summary: Mackenzie Swift entered the River Don in Mexborough on Saturday evening and failed to emerge A body has been found in the search for an 11-year-old boy who went missing after entering the River Don in South Yorkshire on Saturday. Mackenzie Swift entered the river in Mexborough at around 8pm and failed to emerge, prompting a police search. Continue reading...

stories.title: Labour ‘not looking to raise taxes to fund benefits’ as Mandelson messages suggest
stories.summary: Nick Thomas-Symonds defends ‘embarrassing’ Whatsapp messages between Pat McFadden and Mandelson UK politics live – latest updates Labour MPs are not looking to raise taxes to fund more benefits, the Cabinet Office minister Nick Thomas-Symonds has said. In messages between the work and pensions secretary, Pat McFadden, and Peter Mandelson released on Monday, McFadden wrote: “Every meeting I have is: ‘Who can we tax in order to pay benefits to others?’ They’re asking the wrong questions.” Continue

stories.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
stories.summary: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of Tuesday Denmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition. Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Green Left and the centrist Moderates ended two mont

stories.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
stories.summary: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks A far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting. With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a phi

stories.title: French navy boards Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic – video
stories.summary: France has released footage that appears to show French naval commandos boarding a ship suspected of being part of Russia's 'shadow fleet' and subject to international sanctions. The French president, Emmanuel Macron, said the operation took place on the tanker, named the Tagor, with support of the UK and in strict compliance with the law of the sea. The French navy confirmed the interception occurred on Sunday Macron says French navy has boarded Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic Continue rea

stories.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
stories.summary: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’ The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election. That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Co

stories.title: Iran threatens to suspend peace talks after ‘violation of ceasefire’ in Lebanon
stories.summary: Tehran says it holds Israel and US responsible, while Trump says ‘going silent’ on negotiations ‘would be very good’ Iran has indicated it will suspend peace talks with the US in protest against Israel’s offensive in Lebanon, threatening the collapse of negotiations with Washington as the two sides skirmished amid a faltering ceasefire. The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said: “The ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all fronts, including in Lebanon. Its

stories.title: Trump says Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to ‘stop all shooting’
stories.summary: Declaration comes after Iran had pulled out of peace talks in protest at Israeli offensive in Lebanon Donald Trump has said Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to mutual de-escalation and to scale back fighting, seemingly averting an Israeli strike on Beirut and the potential collapse of ceasefire talks with Iran. The US president said in a social media post that he spoke to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both agreed that “all shooting will stop”.

stories.title: EU reaches deal on 'return hubs' for rejected asylum-seekers
stories.summary: The deal involves sending people whose asylum applications have been rejected to third countries. It still requires formal approval to come into effect.

stories.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
stories.summary: Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

stories.title: Seeking deal with Iran, Trump promises calm in Lebanon
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has said Israel and Hezbollah have agreed that "all shooting will stop." Iran has suspended negotiations as it insists there can be no peace that does not include Lebanon.

stories.title: Denmark's Frederiksen secures third term as prime minister
stories.summary: After her party's weakest showing since 1903, Mette Frederiksen spent months negotiating a coalition to stay in power.

stories.title: Most new German citizens keep original nationality: survey
stories.summary: Germany's previous government changed laws to allow dual citizenship. A new survey shows that, one year after that reform, more than 85% of new German citizens are opting to keep their original nationality.

stories.title: Israel-Lebanon conflict: Why military pressure isn't enough
stories.summary: Clashes persist as Israel pushes deeper into Lebanon despite Trump's ceasefire claims. Experts warn that the offensive is undermining talks with Hezbollah and pushing a political solution further out of reach.

stories.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's 'forgotten accounts'?
stories.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in Germany's dormant bank accounts. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

stories.title: European Parliament committee votes to scrap US tariffs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to slap higher tariffs on European goods if the EU misses a July 4 deadline to approve a trade deal with Washington.

stories.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
stories.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

stories.title: Trump inserts himself into the centre of America's 250th birthday celebrations
stories.summary: Instead of a uniting event, America's Independence Day celebrations are becoming yet another partisan battleground.

stories.title: Trump administration will abide by ruling halting $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
stories.summary: The justice department says it "disagrees strongly" with a court decision to halt the establishment of the fund.

stories.title: Toy Story 5 shows 'terror' of children's screen addiction, says Tom Hanks
stories.summary: The latest film sees Woody, Buzz and the other toys compete with a new tablet for children's attention.

stories.title: How health workers in DR Congo are treating Ebola and staying safe
stories.summary: There are currently no approved drugs that target Bundibugyo - the species of Ebola responsible for this outbreak.

stories.title: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
stories.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

stories.title: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
stories.summary: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

stories.title: Missing lab worker found dead in New Mexico nearly a year after disappearing
stories.summary: Melissa Casias' case was part of debunked speculations about recent deaths and disappearances in the US scientific community.

stories.title: US to slash number of embassies in Africa processing visas
stories.summary: Currently there are around 50 US embassies and consulates processing visa applications in Africa, and that number is expected to be significantly reduced to 20 in the coming weeks, according to reports.

stories.title: From Almaty to the skies: Kazakhstan tests Central Asia's first air taxi
stories.summary: Central Asia's first air taxi test flight has taken place in Kazakhstan, as authorities and private partners work to develop a new urban air mobility network. Commercial trial operations are expected by early 2029, subject to certification and regulatory approval.

stories.title: Kyiv residents shelter underground during Russian attack
stories.summary: Residents took refuge in Kyiv’s metro system as Russian missile and drone attacks hit the Ukrainian capital overnight, causing fires, damaging buildings and injuring several people.

stories.title: Pentagon bans journalists from press office, citing classified material
stories.summary: The Pentagon has barred journalists from entering its press office after redesignating the space as a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF), a secure area used for handling classified information.

stories.title: AI giant Anthropic plans to sell shares in US as valuation nears $1tn
stories.summary: The AI company behind Claude is set to offer the public the chance to buy and sell shares in the firm later this year.

stories.title: Kazakhstan open to hosting Iran's uranium stockpile if nuclear deal is reached
stories.summary: Kazakhstan has offered to store Iran’s near-weapons-grade uranium to break the deadlock in US–Iran talks, as IAEA chief Grossi backed the neutral plan.

stories.title: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.title: China goes after 'ghost kitchens' to rein in cut-throat food delivery apps
stories.summary: The thousands of "ghost kitchens" - online shops that don't actually exist - have spooked Chinese consumers.

stories.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
stories.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

stories.title: Why Myanmar president's India visit is being closely watched
stories.summary: Myanmar is seeking to broaden diplomatic engagement after years of international criticism and isolation.

stories.title: Seven killed in Iowa shooting, including gunman
stories.summary: A shooting across several locations in Muscatine, Iowa, has left seven people dead, including the gunman.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire despite Trump announcing breakthrough agreement
stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire after Donald Trump announced a halt, casting doubt on ceasefire efforts.

stories.title: Massive Russian attack on cities across Ukraine kills at least 16 people
stories.summary: An eight-year-old boy and a woman pulled from the rubble of an apartment block are among those killed, officials say.

stories.title: Two people shot dead amid Kenya protests against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
stories.summary: The US plan has sparked public anger and led to demonstrations near the site of the proposed treatment facility.

stories.title: French Navy boards Russia-linked tanker in Atlantic Ocean
stories.summary: Video released by France shows naval forces boarding the Russia-linked tanker Tagor in the Atlantic Ocean. Paris says the vessel was intercepted as part of efforts to enforce sanctions and curb Russian oil exports funding the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Kenya protesters oppose Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Nanyuki against a planned Ebola quarantine centre as Kenya's High Court suspended the project pending review.

stories.title: Tehran suspended negotiations via mediators with US, Iranian media says
stories.summary: Iran has suspended all indirect talks with the US and announced it would pursue "complete closure of Strait of Hormuz," accusing Israel of "continuing crimes" in Lebanon and stating that any ceasefire breach on one front breaks the truce in general.

stories.title: Driver with no right hand ticketed for using phone in her right hand
stories.summary: Bodycam video from Florida shows a police officer issuing a texting-while-driving citation to a woman, despite her not having a right hand. The ticket has since been recommended for dismissal, according to US media reports.

stories.title: US military says it struck a commercial ship trying to breach blockade and reach Iran
stories.summary: According to the US Central Command, the Gambia-flagged cargo ship Lian Star ignored more than 20 warnings from US forces overnight as it tried to enter an Iranian port.

stories.title: ‘What happened to the testicles?’: mockery in Milan over bull mosaic’s restoration
stories.summary: Rampant Bull needed a makeover after wear and tear from tourists, but refurbishment ‘castrated’ it, critics say The restoration of a floor mosaic in Milan called the Rampant Bull has been mocked after the works appear to have erased a crucial anatomical detail – its testicles. The 19th-century mosaic in the Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II shopping arcade needed a makeover because a small crater had formed in the tiny pink tiles featuring the bull’s testicles, due to the constant stream of tourists

stories.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
stories.summary: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned. The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...

stories.title: EasyJet is an obvious takeover target, but US approach may not be a flyer | Nils Pratley
stories.summary: Castlelake’s move raises questions over valuation and ownership rules as well as whether Stelios Haji-Ioannou could throw a spanner in the works EasyJet says US takeover bid would be ‘highly opportunistic’ A share price gain of only 10% on a possible takeover approach is a meek reaction. If the stock market truly believed that Castlelake, a US investment fund, stood a decent chance of buying easyJet , you would expect the target’s stock to fly significantly higher. Scepticism is the right stance

stories.title: Explosion at Malta fireworks factory sends plumes of smoke into air – video
stories.summary: The explosion happened at the Ta' Lourdes factory on the north side of the Mediterranean island on Monday morning. Two men in fields near the factory at the time of the blast were admitted to hospital with minor injuries. Residents in the surrounding area reported shattered windows and buildings shaken by the force of the blast. It is not yet known what caused the explosion Continue reading...

stories.title: Coups and crises shake African democracy
stories.summary: Junta leader Ibrahim Traore's call for Burkina Faso to "forget" democracy signaled a political shift across the continent.

stories.title: Baden-Württemberg restricts smoking in public spaces
stories.summary: The southwestern German state of Baden-Württemberg has expanded restrictions on smoking in public spaces like outdoor swimming pools, theme parks and zoos.

stories.title: London Tube workers go on two-day strike
stories.summary: A strike in London's Tube will see the closure of two train lines and partial closure of two others, as the union demands better conditions for workers.

stories.title: Alphabet plans $80 billion stock sale as AI spending surges
stories.summary: The Google parent company's announcement comes on the same day as Anthropic said it had confidentially filed paperwork for an initial public offering.

stories.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.title: Kyrgyzstan calls for more Asian, African and Latin American seats on UN Security Council
stories.summary: As he announced his country's candidacy for a non-permanent seat on the UN Security Council, Kyrgyzstan's Foreign Minister Jeenbek Kulubaev called for its reform to include greater representation from Asia, Africa and South America.

stories.title: Three Ebola vaccines in development amid growing outbreak fears
stories.summary: IAVI, Moderna and the University of Oxford are all working on new vaccines.

stories.title: US political commentators denied entry to UK by Home Office
stories.summary: The US citizens and left-wing content creators with huge online followings were due to speak at the SXSW London festival and Oxford this week.

stories.title: Italians bemused by Milan bull mosaic restoration
stories.summary: Italians wondered what had happened to the animal's "lucky-charm" testicles.

stories.title: Trump says he secured halt to Israel’s offensive in Lebanon, keeps door open for Iran talks
stories.summary: The US president further eased tensions sparked by Tehran's announcement on Monday of an end to mediated negotiations, saying in a separate post on Truth Social that talks with the Islamic Republic continued "at a rapid pace".

stories.title: OpenAI let ChatGPT aid and abet mass shooters, Florida lawsuit claims
stories.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier alleges OpenAI and boss Sam Altman built a 'web of deceit'

stories.title: ‘I felt a vibration’: Resident describes powerful meteor explosion over Massachusetts
stories.summary: A Massachusetts resident described feeling her apartment shake after a meteor exploded high above New England, releasing energy equivalent to about 300 tons of TNT, according to NASA.

stories.title: Two possible Ebola cases in Brazil ruled out as patients test negative
stories.summary: The two patients had recently returned from the DR Congo and Uganda respectively.

stories.title: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.title: „Büntetőjogi felelőssége tudatában nyilatkozott” – reagált az Állami Számvevőszék a költségvetésből hiányzó 286 milliárd ügyére
stories.summary: Egy titkos kormányhatározatra és egy új kínai hitelre hivatkozott a kormány az ÁSZ-nak küldött tájékoztatásában, amikor a számvevők tavaly az idei költségvetés azon részeiről érdeklődtek, amelyekből mai állás szerint több százmilliárd forint hiányzik.

stories.title: Az olimpián jöttek rá, hogy két országnak ugyanolyan a zászlója
stories.summary: Egymástól 10 ezer kilométerre fekszik Haiti és Liechtenstein, mégis ugyanazt a színkombinációt választották. Változtattak is rajta rögtön.

stories.title: Jogosan került célkeresztbe a bizalmi vagyonkezelés?
stories.summary: Bizalmi vagyonkezelés. Soha nem látott adózási előny! Közpénz! Gazdagok, gazdag cégtulajdonosok! Professzionális vagyonkezelés: jó – családi vagyonkezelés: adóelkerülés. Kiskapu. Nagyon nagy kiskapu!!! Biztosan az? A következőkben ezt vizsgáljuk.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán reagált a szabolcsi fideszes szervezeteknek kiszórt 400 milliónyi NKA-támogatásra
stories.summary: Minisztériuma folytatja az NKA-hoz kapcsolódó anyagok átvilágítását.

stories.summary: Száznál is többen megsebesültek a támadásban, csak Kijevben 63 sérültről számoltak be a híradások.

stories.title: Kedden csak elszórtan lehet zápor, szerdán jön a nagyobb zuhé
stories.summary: A legmagasabb hőmérséklet 23 és 29 fok között alakul kedden.

stories.title: Trump szerint egy héten belül megállapodhatnak Iránnal
stories.summary: Szerinte egy esetleges megállapodás egy katonai győzelemnél is jobb lenne.

stories.title: 1,2 milliárdos nyereséggel zárta az évet a Digitális Szuverenitás Központ mögött álló cég

stories.summary: Az eddigi legnagyobb bevételét érte el 2025-ben a Civil Zrt., Pintér Sándor egykori cége.

stories.summary: A június 2-án történt bombázásban több száz civil is meghalt. Szabó Dénes felvételei a lerombolt utcákat, a túlélők tragédiáját és a város talpra állásának első pillanatait is megörökítették.

stories.title: A Sziget 2,8 milliárd forint mínusszal zárt, de a korábbi tulajdonos rendezte a veszteséget
stories.summary: Már érezhető volt a cég pénzügyein, hogy 2025-ben nem rendezték meg a Balaton Soundot.

stories.summary: Megtette a hatását a választás előtti osztogatás.

stories.title: Sulyok interjút adott egy német lapnak, a Sándor-palota fordításából valahogy kimaradt Novák Katalin neve
stories.summary: „A köztársasági elnöki tisztség méltóságának megőrzése érdekében” sem mondhat le, mondta Sulyok Tamás az interjúban.

stories.title: Több embert bevittek az óbudai korrupciós ügyben
stories.summary: Több önkormányzatnál akciózott a Központi Nyomozó Főügyészség.

stories.summary: Hajós azért bírálta is a TV2-t, egészen pontosan hírműsoraik működését.

stories.title: Egyáltalán nem megnyugtató az MNB-ügy érintettjeinek, ha eddig nem hallgatták meg őket
stories.summary: Kevés meghallgatás, nulla gyanúsítás és zéró kényszerintézkedés történt eddig az MNB-ügy nyomozásában, de 92 milliárd forintot már sikerült lefoglalni. Hogy áll a nyomozás, mit árulnak el az ügyről az eddigi eredmények, és mekkora eséllyel lehet hazahozni egy gyanúsítottat külföldről?

stories.summary: Az Attraction szerint szakmai döntés alapján kapták meg a támogatást, ami többszöröse az éves kiadásaiknak.

stories.summary: A lemondást azzal indokolta, nem akar a továbbiakban az ügynökség operatív feladataival és projektjeivel foglalkozni.

stories.summary: Bérjellegű kiadásokra 2,7 milliárd forintot fordított a Jogállam és Igazság Nonprofit Kft.

stories.summary: A leletek arról tanúskodnak, hogy a mai Normafa helyén egykor vízzel borított völgyek húzódtak.

stories.summary: Az első szerelvények 2029-re érkezhetnek meg.

stories.title: Colombia goes to polls in election pitting outgoing leader’s ally against pro-Trump candidates
stories.summary: Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential elections Colombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict. The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas

stories.title: Clashes in Paris after PSG win Champions League final – video
stories.summary: French police clashed with rioters in the capital after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League title . Hundreds of people were detained across France, with the majority in the Paris area French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win Continue reading...

stories.title: Denmark’s Frederiksen announces government after lengthy talks
stories.summary: Mette Frederiksen is set to return as Danish prime minister by forming a center-left coalition government following negotiations lasting more than two months. The four-party coalition is expected to bring together Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Moderates, Green Left and the Social Liberals, according to the DR public broadcaster. The incoming PM met King Frederik X Monday evening to inform him. “I think everyone will be surprised by how much we want to do. It is a government platform that i

stories.title: Trump ally and leftist candidate head for runoff in Colombia's presidential election
stories.summary: The right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella, an admirer of Donald Trump, came first in the vote, followed closely by left-wing senator Iván Cepeda, backed by current president Gustavo Petro.

stories.title: Iran executes two men over alleged mosque attack as crackdown deepens
stories.summary: Two men were executed after being convicted of setting a Tehran mosque on fire during protests that erupted over the collapse of the rial, as Amnesty International documented 39 political executions and more than 6,000 arrests since the war began in February.

stories.title: New Aukus drone tech to protect critical undersea cables as Marles warns: ‘seabed is a battlefield’
stories.summary: Minister at Singapore defence summit also reveals Australia to buy only secondhand Aukus submarines from US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The defence minister, Richard Marles, has said the “seabed is a battlefield” in a combative speech urging Beijing to be more transparent about its maritime operations, and taking aim at weak international controls over so-called “shadow-fleet” vessels. The warning came as the

stories.title: Ukraine: IAEA seeks access to Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant after reported drone strike
stories.summary: Russia claims that a Ukrainian drone struck the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant. Ukraine has denied the accusation. The International Atomic Energy Agency has sought to examine the facility.

stories.title: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri province Containing the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus , the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against stringent medical protocols for handling victi

stories.title: Yves Sakila’s death has echoes of George Floyd. When will we in Ireland confront our own racism?
stories.summary: Growing up in Dublin, I learned to navigate life in fight-or-flight mode. Yet even now, our leaders are ducking a vital conversation Seán Gallen is a Martinican-Irish writer and film-maker based in Berlin and Dublin Watching the harrowing footage of what would become Yves Sakila’s final moments of consciousness, it is hard not to be reminded of the agonising death of George Floyd. Sakila was declared dead in a Dublin hospital on 15 May, a short time after being pinned to the ground by security g

stories.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study
stories.summary: ‘Megafires’ in California, Canada, South Korea and Europe in 2025, but changes to farming slowed spread in parts of Africa “Devastating” wildfires ripped across the wealthier parts of the world in 2025, a study has found, even as globally, the area ravaged by flames fell. Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. But the 335m hectares burned was the second-lowest since 2002, the review found, largely owing to the expansion of Afric

stories.title: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
stories.summary: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40C A powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices formed within it. This flow can intensify storms. S

A Telexnek adott interjújában arról beszélt, milyen személyes jelentősége volt számára a bemutatónak, hogyan távolodott el évekre a szerzői filmkészítéstől miután az NMHH cenzúrázta a Candide című sorozatot, és hogy mit jelent számára a felnövés.

stories.summary: Hivatalos indoklás nincs, csak annyi, hogy közös megegyezés.

stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás attól az európai szervezettől kért segítséget a mandátuma ügyében, amit a fideszesek korábban sorosista, bevándorláspárti szervezetnek neveztek, és azt mondták, hogy a globalista politikai gépezet része. Igaz, amikor érdekükben állt, akkor elfogadták döntéseit.

stories.title: Az Aggteleki Nemzeti Park medve miatt adott ki figyelmeztetést
stories.summary: Vasárnap és hétfőn is medvét észleltek a környéken.

stories.summary: Trump már üzletemberként is előszeretettel magával akart mindent eladni, a vodkától kezdve parfümön át az épületekig. Második elnöki ciklusában aztán csúcsra járatta az önpromóját, amit sokan ízléstelennek, mások egyenesen autoriter hajlamnak tartanak. A Kennedy Centerről most visszapattant, amit kifakadással fogadott.

stories.title: Keményebb uniós szabályokról állapodtak meg a migránsok visszaküldésére
stories.summary: A kialkudott szöveggel az uniós döntéshozók szerint könnyebben haza tudják majd küldeni azokat, akiknek elutasították a menedékkérelmét, és EU-n kívüli „visszaküldési központokat” hozhatnak létre.

stories.title: Természetes jelenség a madarak önkielégítése, és gyakoribb a vadon élő fajoknál – erre jutott egy friss kutatás
stories.summary: A maszturbáció nem negatív reakció a fogságra.

stories.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
stories.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

stories.title: How the Alevi community came to thrive in Germany
stories.summary: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

stories.title: Germany news: Most expect AfD state premier after elections
stories.summary: Most people in Germany expect a premier from the far-right AfD to lead at least one state after elections in the autumn, a survey has found. The Brenner Pass linking Austria and Italy has reopened.

stories.title: DNA had one rule. Bacteria didn't get the memo
stories.summary: Every cell in every organism on Earth copies DNA the same way. Except one bacterial protein — quietly doing something scientists had never seen before.

stories.title: The German town that prints its own money to cut emissions
stories.summary: It started as a school project to promote local businesses in a remote Bavarian region. Now, the "Chiemgauer" currency helps to cut emissions.

stories.title: Young German talents impress in World Cup tune-up vs Finland
stories.summary: Deniz Undav and Lennart Karl delivered strong performances in Germany's last game at home before the start of 2026 FIFA World Cup.

stories.title: How China keeps North Korea's economy alive
stories.summary: Pyongyang relies on China for 95% of its legitimate trade; hacking, arms deals and remittances also help it stay afloat.

stories.title: Israel retakes Beaufort castle in Lebanon, Paris issues strong condemnation
stories.summary: Israeli forces push deeper into Lebanon and raise their flag over Beaufort Castle, a strategic fortress. France condemns the move and demands a UN Security Council meeting.

stories.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
stories.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.

stories.title: US and Iran exchange fire as peace deal remains in limbo
stories.summary: Washington struck radar and drone sites in Iran over the weekend after Tehran downed a US drone, as Kuwait intercepted Iranian missiles and Trump said a deal "will all work out well in the end".

stories.title: Arsenal fans pack London streets for victory parade
stories.summary: Arsenal fans packed London's streets as the men's Premier League winners and women's champions celebrated a successful season together.

stories.title: Thousands attend annual Israel Day parade in New York
stories.summary: Crowds gathered along New York’s Fifth Avenue for the annual Israel Day parade, waving Israeli and US flags.

stories.title: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.title: Marilyn Monroe 'lookalikes' gather to celebrate her 100th birthday
stories.summary: Fans set a new world record for the largest gathering of people dressed as Marilyn Monroe to celebrate what would have been the legendary actress’ centenary.

stories.title: Litterbugs now face on-the-spot fines in Tokyo's tourist hotspot
stories.summary: Dozens of officials will patrol world-famous Shibuya as Japan responds to the impacts of overtourism.

stories.title: PSG celebrate with Macron at Elysee Palace
stories.summary: Thousands celebrated PSG's Champions League title in Paris as authorities dealt with the fallout from overnight unrest and arrests.

stories.title: Rescuers search for alternative route to reach two missing in flooded Laos cave
stories.summary: The villagers reportedly entered the cave nearly two weeks ago to look for valuable minerals such as gold before being trapped by a flash flood that blocked their way out.

stories.title: Japan’s southernmost region of Okinawa braces for Typhoon Jangmi
stories.summary: The tropical storm, sweeping through the southernmost Japanese prefecture of Okinawa, is expected to head towards Amami Oshima island on Tuesday.

stories.title: Israeli flag raised over Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: An Israeli flag was seen flying over the medieval Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon after Israel said its troops had captured the strategic hilltop fortress.

stories.title: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’ Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance. Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi , Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off – with US encouragement – its pacifist outlook

stories.title: UK, US and Australia to develop 'cutting-edge' underwater drone technology
stories.summary: The new technology is expected to be ready by 2027 and is designed to help defend critical subsea cables and pipelines.

stories.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
stories.summary: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse haza

stories.title: Germany: Vaping becoming more popular among young people
stories.summary: A recent study shows that e-cigarettes are becoming increasingly popular among young people in Germany, with even elementary school kids picking up vapes. Health experts are alarmed.

stories.title: PSG's team of talents retain Champions League title
stories.summary: Paris Saint-Germain were unable to score from open play against a resilient Arsenal but won their second successive title on penalties. Luis Enrique has built a team to be proud of at the oil-rich club.

stories.title: Goodbye, Norma Jeane: How Marilyn Monroe changed Hollywood
stories.summary: Long seen primarily as a sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe is now viewed as an early advocate for women in Hollywood. On the 100th anniversary of her birth, DW looks at the star who balanced fame and feminist agency.

stories.title: ‘Timmy’ the humpback whale hauled ashore on Danish island
stories.summary: The carcass of a humpback whale that drew months of attention in Germany was pulled onto a Danish beach after being found dead in shallow waters. Experts hope an examination will reveal what caused its death.

stories.title: PSG fans erupt in celebration after Champions League triumph
stories.summary: PSG supporters watching the Champions League final on giant screens at Parc des Princes erupted in celebration after their team beat Arsenal on penalties to retain the European crown.

stories.title: Pashinyan on course for landslide victory and pro-West mandate in Armenia election, new poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll projects Armenian Premier Nikol Pashinyan’s party could win a large parliamentary majority, as Russia intensifies its campaign to counter Yerevan's pro-West realignment, warning of a Ukraine scenario.

stories.title: French police detain hundreds in Paris after violent celebrations of PSG’s Champions League title
stories.summary: The French interior ministry said that some 780 persons were questioned nationally, including 283 in Paris alone.

stories.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
stories.summary: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contact

stories.title: Brexiters peddled ‘nationalistic pish’, said Reform UK’s Makerfield candidate
stories.summary: Robert Kenyon castigated Brexit as an economically self-harming project on rugby league forum in 2016 Reform UK’s candidate for the Makerfield byelection has castigated Brexit as an economically self-harming project promoted by politicians who “peddled the nationalistic pish”, raising more doubts about his commitment to Nigel Farage’s signature achievement. The comments by Robert Kenyon, unearthed on a defunct rugby league forum and first reported by the Telegraph, follow the emergence of anothe

stories.title: New food exports deal signals end to Brexit ‘sausage wars’
stories.summary: First details of agreement published, heralding end to EU-UK checks on dairy products, eggs, fish and fresh red meat Business live – latest updates The EU and UK have signalled an end to Brexit “sausage wars” with the first details of a new food exports agreement being published by the British government. The deal will mean no more paperwork or physical checks on dairy, fish, cheese, eggs and fresh red meat from the summer of 2027 for both British exporters to the EU and EU exporters to the UK.

stories.title: Exam fail: Indian students complain en masse about marking errors in key final exams
stories.summary: New digital marking system is aimed at reducing human errors but many students say it has resulted in wrong grades A national outcry has erupted in India after more than 400,000 students requested copies of their answer sheets amid mounting complaints of errors in the marking of the country’s most important school-leaving examinations. Within days of the grade 12 exam results being issued, students began reporting marking discrepancies they linked to a new digital marking system. Continue readin

stories.title: Reiche brings energy sector ties to German Economy Ministry
stories.summary: Economy Minister Reiche is a controversial figure. Her plans for new gas-fired power plants, rolling back plans for more renewables in the building sector and scrapping solar subsidies have been met with criticism.
